title: Trigonometry, Vectors, and Periodic Systems · Model Lens: Sinusoidal Fit
summary: Plan bearings, slopes, cyclic demand, and resultant forces. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math int sinusoidal fit increment in a navigation and periodic-demand model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Fit amplitude, period, phase, and midline to a periodic dataset.
Resolve vectors into components and reconstruct magnitude and direction.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Calculate Sinusoidal Fit evidence
steps.instruction: Perpendicular components are 3 units and 4 units. Calculate the resultant magnitude.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Use √(3² + 4²) and check that the result exceeds either component.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Use √(3² + 4²) and check that the result exceeds either component.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes units.
